description: 41 - 19-12-2024 - Emails between Sir Philip Barton (former Permanent Under-Secretary, ECDO) and others
text:
ECDO) and others

From: Rochelle Fisher Personal

Sent: Thur 19/12/2024 9:46:09 PM (UTC)
To: Rochelle Fisher[Personal

Subject: Re: Main thread - Senior Appointment
Sent from Outlook for iOS

Amended

JCS

On the 'has Trump agreed’

Can we say 'the usual approval process has been followed' rather than ‘the usual appointments
process has been followed’ to be correct?

Thanks
Rochelle

Sent from Outlook for iOS

From: Philip R Barton

Sent: Thursday, December 19, 2024 9:08:03 PM

To: JCS ; Ailsa Terry (No.10) ; James Roscoe ; Caroline Hurndall ; Janine Lloyd-Jones ;
Rochelle Fisher ; JCS ; Robert Tinline

Subject: Re: Main thread - Senior Appointment

OFFICIAL SENSITIVE

Thank JCS

Ailsa and I discussed lines on exact timing earlier today and agreed we'd stick to "early in the
New Year" and not be drawn on exactly when until we have a firm plan.

Philip
Sent from Outlook for iOS
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE
From: JCS

Sent: Thursday, December 19, 2024 7:16 pm

129

To: Ailsa Terry (No.10) ; James Roscoe ; Philip R Barton ; Caroline Hurndall ; Janine Lloyd-Jones
; Rochelle Fisher ; JGS ; Robert Tinline
Subject: RE: Main thread - Senior Appointment

You don't often get email from Personal Learn why this is
important
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE

Hiall - here's current draft of the Q&A.

@Rochelle Fisher would you mind sending me the relevant section of the CRGA? Thank you so much,
Please can you advise on any edits needed, or what I’ve missed? I know we need more on his background
(links with China etc) but I'll pull that together in the morning - just focusing on the process for now.
Thanks

O&A
‘When does he start?
He will take up his post in January 2025.
Will he be in post ahead of inauguration?
That is the expectation, but we will set this out in due course.
‘Whats his status - will he be a Spad?
No ~ the role remains a civil service post.
‘Who made the appointment?
This was a political appointment, which is provided for under the Constitutional Reform and Governance
Act 2010.
Tt was approved by the Prime Minister and the Foreign Secretary.
So why not appoint a civil servant? Is there previous precedent for political appointments to HMA roles?
It is not unusual to make political appointments in the diplomatic service. This is provided for under the
Constitutional Reform and Govemance Act 2010.
Previous political appointees as Heads of Mission in recent years include:
* Alastair Goodlad (British High Commissioner to Australia 2000-2005)
* Paul Boateng (British High Commissioner to South Africa 2005 — 2009)
* Helen Liddell (British High Commissioner to Australia 2005 — 2009)
* Baroness Amos (British High Commissioner to Australia 2009 — 2010)
* Ed Llewellyn (Ambassador to France 2016-2022 and then Ambassador to Italy)
* George Hollingberry (current HMA to Cuba)

First Jonathan Powell, now Peter Mandelson - just appointing friends to top jobs?

All appointments are made on the basis of having the right skills and experience for the role. Lord
Mandelson is a highly respected and experienced figure who will bring the required skills to the role as we
build relationships with the new administration and continue to strengthen our partnership.

Tt is not unusual to make political appointments in the diplomatic service and this is permitted under the
Constitutional Reform and Governance Act 2010.

When does Karen leaye?

As we have previously set out, she will leave her post in January 2025. [Can go further here if it’s
confirmed?]

130


Why are you letting her go when she holds all the relationships with the Trump team?

Karen has completed her four years in the role, which is the usual duration for an Ambassador posting, and
we have always said she will move on in January 2025 - this follows an extension to her original contract
in order to lead the Embassy through the recent U.S. election.

The Prime Minster has thanked her for her outstanding service in her role.

Has Trump agreed?

The usual appointments process has been followed.

We have informed both the current U.S. administration and the incoming administration. [any further we can
go here would be helpful - think this is being handled on the No 10 side though]

Has Foreign Secretary been informed?

Yes, he approved the appointment.

Palace informed?

Yes, in the usual way and the King approved the appointment on the recommendation of the Prime Minister
and Foreign Secretary.

Why not a current civil servant?

It is perfectly reasonable for political appointments to be made to diplomatic roles - there is previous
precedent for this.

On background: Obviously this is a very important time for the UK-US relationship and Lord Mandelson is a
highly respected and experienced figure who will bring the required skills to the role as we build
relationships with the new administration and continue to strengthen our partnership.

What will he be paid?

0]

Has he giyen up his business interests?

His appointment will be subject to the usual processes which are ongoing, including declarations and any
requisite advice and mitigations, as required.

What about his links with China? ee

OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE

From: Ailsa Terry
Sent: Thursday, December 19, 2024 5:49 PM
To: JCS ; James Roscoe : Philip R Barton ; Caroline Hurndall : Janine Lloyd-
Jones ; Rochelle Fisher ; JCS ; Robert Tinline
Subject: Re: Main thread - Senior Appointment
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE

Thank you.

We need to hold telling staff until I give the green light - I've said same to Karen.

We will do so in time for staff to hear from us before they hear it from the media.

Please don’t inform staff until we say we are ready. I’ll come back on timing shortly.
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE

after and we aren't making the announcement until tomorrow morning. Can we discuss that?

Thanks so much,
Jcs

OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE

Glasgow. Back online c1815.

Philip

Subject: RE: Main thread - Senior Appointment
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE
Hi 
Colleagues may have a view on terminology but I think we are ‘ appointing’ , subject to 
the conclusion of bilateral processes.
Head of Mission roles are usually advertised across Government. The FCDO's 
recruitment processes are underpinned by the principle of selection for appointment 
on merit, on the basis of fair and open competition. Recommendations for 
appointment for these roles are ratified by the Foreign Secretary and, for the most 
senior posts, the Prime Minister. Appointments are then approved by HMTK.
The Constitutional Reform and Governance (CRAG) Act 2010 is the statutory basis 
for ensuring that Civil Service recruitment is fair, open and based on merit. Section 10 
(3) of the Constitutional Reform and Governance Act allows for Head of Mission
appointments to take place outside of the CSC’ s rules and allows for political
appointments to be made to Head of Mission roles (or Governorships). Such
appointments are few in number but have been used by successive administrations
since 1945.
Best wishes
Rochelle
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE

Some background on the usual process we follow for Ambassador appointments would be very useful too. 
Likewise, are there any previous examples of administrations making political appointments to Ambassador 
roles?
Thanks very much,

Dear all,

JCS and I just spoke which was very helpful.
We are working to the choreography below:
1. Awaiting Palace approval: pls let us know asap when you get this
2. Morgan is having the calls with the US side
3. @Philip R Barton tells Karen who is it is
4, James tells staff in BE Washington at UK time 1900hrs @James Roscoe
5. No 10 gives the story to the Times at UK time 2000 hours
6. FCDO does the usual conflict of interest assessment (takes a few days), PET standing by
to support. @JCS checking if PM independent adviser would want to review
7. FCDO or No 10 release an gov.uk statement (tbc @JCS
8. @Philip R Barton takes forward arrangements with the individual on T&Cs, salary etc
signposting to James, USCAD as necessary

Ailsa

‘When does he start?
He will take up his post in January 2025.
Will he be in post ahead of inauguration?
That is the expectation, but we will set this out in due course.
‘Whats his status - will he be a Spad?
No ~ the role remains a civil service post.
‘Who made the appointment?
This was a political appointment, which is provided for under the Constitutional Reform and Governance
Act 2010.
Tt was approved by the Prime Minister and the Foreign Secretary.
So why not appoint a civil servant? Is there previous precedent for political appointments to HMA roles?
It is not unusual to make political appointments in the diplomatic service. This is provided for under the
Constitutional Reform and Govemance Act 2010.
Previous political appointees as Heads of Mission in recent years include:
* Alastair Goodlad (British High Commissioner to Australia 2000-2005)
* Paul Boateng (British High Commissioner to South Africa 2005 — 2009)
* Helen Liddell (British High Commissioner to Australia 2005 — 2009)
* Baroness Amos (British High Commissioner to Australia 2009 — 2010)
* Ed Llewellyn (Ambassador to France 2016-2022 and then Ambassador to Italy)
* George Hollingberry (current HMA to Cuba)

First Jonathan Powell, now Peter Mandelson - just appointing friends to top jobs?

All appointments are made on the basis of having the right skills and experience for the role. Lord
Mandelson is a highly respected and experienced figure who will bring the required skills to the role as we
build relationships with the new administration and continue to strengthen our partnership.

Tt is not unusual to make political appointments in the diplomatic service and this is permitted under the
Constitutional Reform and Governance Act 2010.

When does Karen leaye?

As we have previously set out, she will leave her post in January 2025. [Can go further here if it’s
confirmed?]

130


Why are you letting her go when she holds all the relationships with the Trump team?

Karen has completed her four years in the role, which is the usual duration for an Ambassador posting, and
we have always said she will move on in January 2025 - this follows an extension to her original contract
in order to lead the Embassy through the recent U.S. election.

The Prime Minster has thanked her for her outstanding service in her role.

Has Trump agreed?

The usual appointments process has been followed.

We have informed both the current U.S. administration and the incoming administration. [any further we can
go here would be helpful - think this is being handled on the No 10 side though]

Has Foreign Secretary been informed?

Yes, he approved the appointment.

Palace informed?

Yes, in the usual way and the King approved the appointment on the recommendation of the Prime Minister
and Foreign Secretary.

Why not a current civil servant?

It is perfectly reasonable for political appointments to be made to diplomatic roles - there is previous
precedent for this.

On background: Obviously this is a very important time for the UK-US relationship and Lord Mandelson is a
highly respected and experienced figure who will bring the required skills to the role as we build
relationships with the new administration and continue to strengthen our partnership.

What will he be paid?

0]

Has he giyen up his business interests?

His appointment will be subject to the usual processes which are ongoing, including declarations and any
requisite advice and mitigations, as required.

What about his links with China? ee

Head of Mission roles are usually advertised across Government. The FCDO's 
recruitment processes are underpinned by the principle of selection for appointment 
on merit, on the basis of fair and open competition. Recommendations for 
appointment for these roles are ratified by the Foreign Secretary and, for the most 
senior posts, the Prime Minister. Appointments are then approved by HMTK.
The Constitutional Reform and Governance (CRAG) Act 2010 is the statutory basis 
for ensuring that Civil Service recruitment is fair, open and based on merit. Section 10 
(3) of the Constitutional Reform and Governance Act allows for Head of Mission
appointments to take place outside of the CSC’ s rules and allows for political
appointments to be made to Head of Mission roles (or Governorships). Such
appointments are few in number but have been used by successive administrations
since 1945.
Best wishes
Rochelle

next Ambassador or are we‘nominating'?
Some background on the usual process we follow for Ambassador appointments would be very useful too. 
Likewise, are there any previous examples of administrations making political appointments to Ambassador 
roles?
Thanks very much,

messages.body:
Dear all,

JCS and I just spoke which was very helpful.
We are working to the choreography below:
1. Awaiting Palace approval: pls let us know asap when you get this
2. Morgan is having the calls with the US side
3. @Philip R Barton tells Karen who is it is
4, James tells staff in BE Washington at UK time 1900hrs @James Roscoe
5. No 10 gives the story to the Times at UK time 2000 hours
6. FCDO does the usual conflict of interest assessment (takes a few days), PET standing by
to support. @JCS checking if PM independent adviser would want to review
7. FCDO or No 10 release an gov.uk statement (tbc @JCS
8. @Philip R Barton takes forward arrangements with the individual on T&Cs, salary etc
signposting to James, USCAD as necessary

Ailsa
